rulings.Case Title: PAUL R BARNES VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDENESDAY, DECEMBER 1, 2010 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 1, Defendant American Sterilizer co.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. After considering the parties¿ supplemental briefing, defendant did not have notice of Mr. Barnes¿ deposition and thus the court reaffirms the prior tentative ruling that ¿Defendant sustained its burden of production and plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether Mr. Barnes was exposed to asbestos-containing materials or products from an American Sterilizer autoclave. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Barnes¿ deposition and Mr. Cohen¿s declaration are sustained.¿ = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: STEVEN PETERSON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, DECEMBER 1, 2010 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 2, Defendant Air & Liquid Systems successor by merger to Buffalo Pumps¿ motion for summary judgment is granted. No opposition filed. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM TORCHIA VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, DECEMBER 1, 2010 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 6, Defendant Underground Construction Company, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Plaintiff¿s deposition testimony and declaration create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing insulation attributable disturbed by defendant. Defendant¿s objections to plaintiff¿s declaration are overruled. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: BRUCE GLEASON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, DECEMBER 1, 2010 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 7, Defendant The Toro Co.¿s motion for summary judgment and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to plaintiff¿s exposure to asbestos attributable to defendant and granted as to false representation and punitive damages. Even assuming the admissibility of the declarations of Mr. Christopherson and Ms. Ring, plaintiff¿s deposition testimony and declaration and the declaration of Dr. Bruch create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos attirubatable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to plaintiff¿s declaration are overruled. Plaintiff concedes the lack of merit of his claim for false representation and request for punitive damages. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CHERYL HOSKINS et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBIRT et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, DECEMBER 1, 2010 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 3, Defendant Atlas Heating & Ventilating Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is off calendar at the moving party¿s request. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM MECHLING VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, DECEMBER 1, 2010 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 4, Defendant Tosco Refining Co.¿s motion for summary adjudication is denied. Assuming without deciding that the issue of special employment is subject to summary adjudication notwithstanding the rule that only an entire claim may be summarily adjudicated and that the deposition testimony of Mr. Holz is admissible, plaintiff¿s deposition testimony creates a triable issue whether he was the special employee of defendant. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLEY BROWN VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, DECEMBER 1, 2010 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 5, Defendant Campbell Industries¿ motion for summary judgment and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to the first three issues (statute of limitations, workers¿ compensation exclusivity and identification of defendant in a non-employment capacity) and taken off calendar as to the last three issues (strict liability, false representation and punitive damages). Defendant did not satisfy its burden of production that plaintiff does not have asbestosis. Defendant¿s workers¿ compensation argument is not amenable to summary adjudication since it does not dispose of an entire claim. Plaintiff¿s non-employment identification of defendant is not speculative. Plaintiff dismissed his claims for strict liability and false representation and his request for punitive damages. = (220/HEK)
